title: abutilon fraseri subsp. fraseri
intro: Abutilon fraseri subsp. fraseri, also known as the Fraser's abutilon or Fraser's hollyhock mullein, is a flowering plant species in the family Malvaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, making it useful in treating conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant's health benefits also extend to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat infections and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Abutilon fraseri subsp. fraseri, such as infusions and topical creams, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
